etymology.description: Originally 止(止) (zhǐ, "foot") + 王(王) (wáng, "king") = phonetic for pronunciation -> 彳(彳) (chì, "step") added for movement -> right component stylized to unrelated 主(主) (zhǔ, "lord") forming final character
etymology.interpretation: The character 往(wǎng) incorporates semantic and phonetic elements: 彳(chì, "step") suggests movement and 止(zhǐ, "foot") indicates direction, while 王(wáng, "king") originally provided the pronunciation, which was later obscured as the character evolved.

examples.chinese: 他往门口走(他往門口走)。
我们往东边旅行(我們往東邊旅行)。
她常常回忆往事(她常常回憶往事)。
examples.english: She often reminisces about the past.
